discussion_posts.name: lovesbumblebees
discussion_posts.content:
HINT:
- when looking for comma positions you need both substrings to be valid. Your substring is valid if and only if one of the following holds:
   - it does not start with 0
   - it starts with 0 and does not end with 0
   - it is one digit long
- when looking for decimal positions within a substring, you must follow these rules:
   - a string of length 1 has no decimal
   - if the string starts with 0, then a decimal MUST immediately follow
   - if the string ends with 0, then no decimal may appear anywhere
   - if none of the above hold, then a decimal can appear between any two digits

discussion_posts.name: notneasd
discussion_posts.content:
I think backtracking makes it unnecessarily complicated here. Instead:

- first create all possible splits (i.e. your comma)
- then for every pair (x,y) from the splits, get the valid coordinates for x and for y respectively. (your chance to put optional dots)
- use O(n^2) loop to concatenate all valid coordiantes pair together.
